economic_notes: First Nation operates Power Authority providing electrical power to community, Economic Development segment includes small business centre. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership (Casino Rama funds) generates revenue. Community is economically dependent on ISC funding per treaties.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on three bases: (1) inability to observe/verify physical inventories at beginning and end of year; (2) inability to verify completeness of energy sales and related receivables due to meter reading deficiencies; (3) unrecorded tangible capital assets of unknown extent with related amortization undetermined. Power Authority energy sales receivable of $4,115,487 fully offset by allowance for doubtful accounts. Demand loan of $1,000,000 was fully repaid during the year leaving zero long-term debt. No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text. Ministry of Northern Development and Mines budget of $75,002 shows $0 actual. First Nation did not comply with First Nations Financial Transparency Act posting deadline. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $17,279,445 + Province of Ontario $2,595,599 + CMHC $37,500 + Ministry of Indigenous Relations & Reconciliation $68,750 + Nishnawbe Aski Nation $281,994 + Keewaytinook Okimakanak $249,325 + Sioux Lookout Area Aboriginal Management Board $107,051 - Recovery of funding $131,049 (net) = approximately $20,488,615 before netting recovery; OSR includes energy sales, OFNLP distributions and other non-government revenue.
